codeReview.summary: Rollback fault-type scoping is implemented correctly, but RestartService still does not clear all faults end-to-end: bad_config remains in SimulatedSystem active fault tracking after restart, so incidents stay stale.

issues.description: _action_restart() restores health/metrics but never removes target_service from self._active_faults. Repro (focused check): inject bad_config on db, run RestartService(db), then env._system._active_faults is still {'db': 'bad_config'} and env.state.active_incidents still includes 'bad_config on db'. This violates expected behavior that restart clears all faults on target service.

test_restart_clears_all_faults_on_service only asserts chaos-engine faults are cleared and db health is true; it does not assert system._active_faults/active_incidents are cleared. This misses the stale bad_config fault state and allows a false pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The env-worker skill procedure requires reading `.factory/research/openenv-api.md` and `.factory/library/architecture.md` first, but this worker transcript skeleton does not show either file read.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 38fed58e-e883-4f33-b080-02949cb697a2 shows reads of mission files plus `Read` on `.factory/library` (directory), but no read call for `.factory/research/openenv-api.md` or `.factory/library/architecture.md`.

summary: Review status: fail. The rollback over-clear fix is mostly correct, but a blocking correctness gap remains: RestartService does not fully clear bad_config from system fault tracking, so incidents remain stale after restart. A related test coverage gap allowed this to pass.
